enonce.texte:
Read the sentences below carefully. Each sentence describes a situation related to community service. Fill in the blank with the most appropriate word from the list provided.

Word List: volunteer, community, benefits, skills, experience, project, impact, commitment

questions.texte: Describe the primary motivation for someone who chooses to work without financial reward.
questions.reponse: A volunteer is someone who chooses to work without financial reward.

sousQuestions.texte: A ______ is a person who freely offers to do something, often for an organization or event, without being paid.
Working with others on a local clean-up helps to strengthen the sense of belonging and mutual support within a ______.
One of the main ______ of volunteering is gaining new abilities and meeting people who share your values.
Through volunteering, you can develop valuable ______ like leadership, communication, and teamwork that are useful for your future career.
Her previous ______ as a tutor at the local orphanage helped her decide to become a teacher.

intitule: Expressing Purpose with 'to' and 'in order to'

enonce.texte: A group of students is explaining why they volunteer. Combine the two ideas in each sentence to form one sentence. Use to + infinitive or in order to + infinitive.

questions.texte: Combine the sentences: Yannick participates in the 'Clean Your City' campaign. He wants to reduce plastic waste in his neighborhood.
questions.reponse: Yannick participates in the 'Clean Your City' campaign to reduce plastic waste in his neighborhood.

questions.texte: Combine the sentences: Helene teaches mathematics to underprivileged children. She wants to help them pass the Baccalaureate exam.
questions.reponse: Helene teaches mathematics to underprivileged children in order to help them pass the Baccalaureate exam.

questions.texte: Combine the sentences: The students organized a fundraising event. They wanted to buy new books for the school library.
questions.reponse: The students organized a fundraising event to buy new books for the school library.

questions.texte: Combine the sentences: The NGO built a new well in the village. They aimed to provide access to clean drinking water for everyone.
questions.reponse: The NGO built a new well in the village in order to provide access to clean drinking water for everyone.

enonce.texte:
Read the text carefully.

My Year as a Volunteer Teacher

After my Baccalaureate, I had no clear idea of what I wanted to study. My uncle suggested I try volunteering with 'Teach for Cameroon.' I was hesitant at first because I wanted to earn money for my university fees. However, I decided to apply. They sent me to a small village school in the Adamawa region.

The first month was a challenge. The school had no library, and many students shared one textbook. I had to be creative. I used bottle caps to teach counting and local stories to teach reading. I remember a student named Aissatou. She was very shy and struggled to write her name. I worked with her every day after class. By the end of the year, she wrote a whole paragraph about her family. Seeing her smile gave me more joy than any paycheck could.

That year didn't just help Aissatou; it changed me. I learned patience, problem-solving, and how to lead a group. When I finally went to university to study Sociology, I was far ahead of my classmates. I had real-world experience. Volunteering isn't just about giving. It is an investment in your own future. I urge every young person to consider it.

questions.texte: Identify the main problem the narrator faced during his first month of volunteering.
questions.reponse: The main problem was a severe lack of learning materials. The school had no library, and many students shared one textbook.

questions.texte: According to the text, justify how the narrator solved the problem of limited resources.
questions.reponse: He solved the problem by being creative. He used bottle caps to teach counting and local stories to teach reading.

questions.texte: Analyze the narrator's statement: "Seeing her smile gave me more joy than any paycheck could." What does this reveal about the value of volunteering?
questions.reponse: This statement reveals that the intrinsic, non-material rewards of volunteering (like seeing a student succeed, feeling a sense of purpose and accomplishment) can be more valuable and fulfilling than financial compensation. It highlights the emotional and psychological benefits of helping others.

questions.texte: Based on the text, formulate a definition of volunteering that includes its benefits for both the helper and the community.
questions.reponse: Volunteering is a selfless act of offering one's time and skills to a community or cause without expecting financial reward. Its benefits are mutual: the community receives valuable help (e.g., education, resources), while the volunteer gains crucial life skills (patience, leadership), experience, and personal fulfillment.

intitule: Designing a Volunteer Project for Your School

enonce.texte: You are the president of your school's Social Club. A recent study shows that many students in your lycée feel isolated and that cases of bullying are increasing. Your club wants to launch a volunteer project to solve this problem and create a more inclusive environment. Use the grammar and vocabulary from the lesson to complete the task.

questions.texte: Plan your project by answering the questions below. Write complete sentences.

sousQuestions.texte: State the name and the main goal of your project. (Use 'in order to' or 'to')
sousQuestions.reponse: The project is called 'Together We Stand' in order to create a more inclusive and friendly school environment and to reduce bullying.
sousQuestions.critere: Correct use of purpose clause and clear objective.

sousQuestions.texte: Describe two concrete activities that student volunteers will organize. (Use vocabulary: skills, community, benefits, experience)
sousQuestions.reponse: First, volunteers will organize weekly 'Buddy Circles' where older students use their listening skills to support isolated younger students. Second, the volunteers will launch a 'Peer Mentorship' program so that students who have experience resolving conflicts can help their peers.
sousQuestions.critere: Two distinct activities using at least two vocabulary words from the list.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain one long-term benefit for a student who joins your project as a volunteer. (Use the present perfect tense)
sousQuestions.reponse: A student who joins this project will be able to say, 'I have developed strong leadership and empathy skills that will help me in my future career.'
sousQuestions.critere: Correct use of present perfect tense to describe an acquired experience.
